titleStmt.title: SII 41.105: original edition by S. Rajagopal
No. 105. (A.R. No. 104 of 1905.) Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District. On the slab built into the floor of the first circuit of the Erichchā-Uḍaiyār temple at Ambāsamudram.

p.hi: No king. Year 39 = c. 10th century A.D.

summary.p: In Vaṭṭeḻuttu. Gift of 150 sheep for three lamps in the temple of Tiruppōttuḍaiya-bhaṭārar in Iḷaṅgōkkuḍi, a brahmadēya in Muḷḷi-nāḍu by three ladies, Taviriṇi Sātaṇan Antari of Kulattūr in Muḷḷināḍu, Iraiyāṉ Iraisiṅgam of Māṇilai-Veṇmāṉiḍam in Teṉ-Kaḷavaḻi-nāḍu and her younger sister Iraiyāṉ Nōṇpay.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī yāṇṭu muppattoṉpatu Ivvāṇṭu muḷḷināṭṭup pira
mateyam Iḷaṅkoykkuṭi tiruppottuṭaiya bhaṭārarkku Innāṭṭu
kuḷattūr taviṇiri cātaṇaṉ Antari vaicca tiruviḷakku Oṉṟu teṉkaḷavaḻi nāṭṭu
māṇilai veṇmāṉiṭam Iraiyāṉ Iraiciṅkam vaicca viḷakku Oṉṟu Ivaḷ taṅkai
Iraiyāṉoṇpay vaicca viḷakku Oṉṟu Ākaviḷakku mūṉṟu Immūṉṟu viḷakkiṉukku
niyati muḻakkuneyyaṭṭi Erivatāka vaiytta cāvāmūvā perāṭu nūṟṟaimpatu
Digital edition of SII 41.105 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
